meta.name: resume
meta.notes: focus on web3 and more authentic to me

basics.name: Joe Black
basics.label: Senior backend software engineer & web3 enthusiast
basics.summary: Innovative Senior Backend Engineer with 10+ years of experience architecting and scaling high-performance systems that support millions of users. Expert in Python and blockchain technologies with a proven track record of delivering critical backend infrastructure across fintech, media, and healthcare sectors. Combines technical leadership with mentorship capabilities, having successfully led engineering teams to deliver enterprise-grade web3 solutions generating multiple millions in revenue. Known for building robust, secure systems with cryptographic protocols and optimizing API performance while reducing infrastructure costs. Seeking roles where my expertise in distributed systems and decentralized technologies can drive meaningful product evolution.

awards.title: Winner
awards.summary: Stanford Datajam, a hackathon sponsored by the US Department of Education.

work.name: Magic Labs, Inc
work.summary: Series-B startup web3 IaaS provider of non-custodial wallets and passwordless authentication.
highlights: Led backend services and API development for Magic SDK, scaling platform to support 1M+ monthly users while architecting optimization tools that improved endpoint performance by 5-10x and reduced infrastructure costs by ~25%.

Architected and developed Wallet-as-a-Service product enabling enterprise web3 adoption with clients including PayPal, Clubhouse, Mattel, Macy's, 7-Eleven, and Forbes, directly contributing millions in annual recurring revenue.

Created essential developer tooling ecosystem (CRUD Router, CLI Router, Factor Verifier, Deferred Calls, ORM Signals) that reduced feature development time by approximately 50% while improving code quality and reducing technical debt.

Mitigated phishing attacks by designing and implementing a device-based cryptographic root-of-trust protocol securing over 3M devices, resulting in pending patent application for "Anonymous Device Fingerprinting for Device Verification".

Mentored 8-10 backend engineers across multiple teams through structured 1:1s, pairing sessions, technical design reviews, and establishing comprehensive engineering documentation covering Python style guides, API standards, and testing best practices.

work.name: Code & Theory
work.summary: Digital-first agency where my project was to work on the CNN API/Datacloud project.
highlights: Architected and scaled CNN Datacloud from proof-of-concept to production, processing 150M+ political data points spanning 100 years and 30+ dimensions to power John King's Magic Wall for the 2020 election night broadcast viewed by millions.

Implemented critical ETL pipelines using Airflow and Neo4j that processed 100K+ daily ingests from diverse sources, integrating FEC financial summaries and multi-dimensional polling data with zero tolerance for errors during live broadcasts.

Developed a high-performance geospatial-temporal microservice handling 20,000+ requests per minute for real-time election donation heat-maps and representative lookups by location and date, critical for CNN's election center website.

Enhanced a Neomodel-based object graph mapper with custom capabilities that automatically generated comprehensive markdown documentation from model metadata, reducing documentation effort by approximately 100 hours annually.

Led a cross-continental technical mentorship program for 8 Python engineers of various experience levels, improving team productivity by 30% across 5 critical projects while establishing consistent coding standards.

work.name: See-Thru Healthcare
work.summary: Pre-Seed, health-tech startup building a transparent marketplace for behavior health telemedicine.
highlights: Led architecture and development for a behavioral health tech startup (5-person team, seed-funded) building a group tele-therapy platform that served 1,000+ members and drove thousands of new providers to our core marketplace product.

Made strategic technology decisions to maximize productivity of our 2-person development team, including serverless cloud architecture (AWS Lambda/Zappa) and Zoom integration for video, resulting in 30% faster development cycles.

Designed and implemented a GraphQL/Relay API using Python (Flask, Graphene) that enabled more modular, declarative, and reusable components in our React.js frontend, significantly improving application maintainability.

Architected a unified OAuth identity solution using AWS Cognito that streamlined user registration and authentication across products, successfully onboarding 1,000+ users while directing growth to our core marketplace.

work.name: Telephone
highlights: Co-founded a pre-ICO blockchain startup, leading extensive research and development of a decentralized communications protocol on Ethereum comparable to Signal but with enhanced privacy features.

Architected and implemented a secure messaging system using Solidity, Python, and JavaScript that leveraged Postal Service over Swarm (PSS) dark-routing capabilities to prevent traffic analysis and protect user identity for 10,000+ potential users.
Designed smart contracts for message integrity verification and secure multi-node media relaying, ensuring IP address protection and enabling offline message retrieval without compromising security.

Created a decentralized services marketplace supporting 100+ anticipated services that incentivized developer contributions through a token economy, driving community growth and platform adoption while maintaining core privacy principles.

volunteer.summary: Mentored hackers and students from local universities and code bootcamps with various engineering related projects and tasks.
Delivered a bio-hacking talk discussing the usage of various technologies, proteins, and growth factors for cognitive enhancement with the co-founder of Hack Miami.

projects.name: BTCPay-python
projects.description: The official python client for the BTCPay Server API, an open-source payment processor for bitcoin and litecoin.

projects.name: coinaddr
projects.description: Cryptocurrency address inspection/validation library for python

projects.name: Pricing
projects.description: A python package for aggregating, converting, and formatting of prices in a variety of currencies, including crypto and custom.

projects.name: TmplD
projects.description: Advanced configuration templating in jinja2 using cluster state for self-configuring, containerized, microservice architectures.
